metadata_json.notes: Model grader's claims are substantiated by the expert_memo preview and the trace's embedded_packet values. The grader correctly documented the inability to programmatically inspect the .xlsx (openpyxl unavailable) and used the memo for semantic checks; that limitation is noted and transparent. No evidence of hidden-reference leakage was found.

extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: Artifacts present and memo content grounded in previews and trace; two issues: (1) XLSX binary could not be rendered in this environment (openpyxl unavailable) so visual cell-level verification is incomplete; (2) minor internal inconsistency where the deterministic precheck marks semantic correctness as not evaluated but the model grader asserts a semantic score of 1.0.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

issues.description: The artifact_content_previews show the XLSX file parse_status as openpyxl_unavailable with preview_error_message 'No module named \u0027openpyxl\u0027'. The trace documents manual OOXML package generation and package-level inspection (workbook XML, cached values, formulas). Because no spreadsheet renderer was available, the grader could not perform a cell-level visual verification; instead validation relied on XML-level checks and builder execution trace. This limits the ability to confirm visible formula placement and exact in-GUI behavior.
issues.recommendation: Have a human verifier or CI step open flow_gating_1.xlsx in a standard spreadsheet client (or provide a rendered spreadsheet preview) to confirm visible formulas and cell-level values match the memo.
issues.short: Spreadsheet binary not renderable in this environment

issues.description: Deterministic precheck metadata and explicit notes state 'semantic_correctness_not_evaluated', yet the model grader outputs include semantic_score/semantic_correctness_score = 1.0 in multiple places (raw_model_output, raw_llm_output). This is an internal consistency issue: either semantic evaluation was performed by the model (contradicting the deterministic precheck note) or the semantic score was asserted without full independent verification. The grader does include limitations acknowledging reliance on memo text and package-level checks, but the deterministic precheck and model-scored semantic result are not reconciled.
issues.recommendation: Clarify whether semantic correctness was intentionally judged by the model and document the exact basis (memo-only vs. full workbook cell inspection). If semantic scoring is retained, add a short justification mapping memo statements and XML-confirmed cached values to the specific rubric criteria used for the semantic score.

recommended_actions: Open the XLSX in a spreadsheet client to visually verify formulas and cached values
Clarify semantic-scoring provenance and align deterministic_precheck notes with model semantic claims

issues.description: Deterministic precheck metadata and explicit notes state 'semantic_correctness_not_evaluated', yet the model grader outputs include semantic_score/semantic_correctness_score = 1.0 in multiple places (raw_model_output, raw_llm_output). The grader does include limitations acknowledging reliance on memo text and package-level checks, but the deterministic precheck and model-scored semantic result are not reconciled.
